{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Network Admin Assistant",
  "description": "Sophia ERP Limited (SEL) is an Enterprise Solutions Development Organization, on a mission to power Governments and Businesses with cost effective and suitable Digital Transformation solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Network Admin Assistant

Job Location: Ojodu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking to hire a proactive and detail-oriented Network Admin Assistant to support the management, maintenance, and security of our technology infrastructure and daily operations.
	The ideal candidate will assist in ensuring systems and network services run efficiently, support software testing activities, provide timely technical and administrative support to internal teams, and assist with basic IT training initiatives for staff.


Responsibilities
IT &amp; Systems Support:


	Monitor network, server, and system performance to ensure optimal availability and efficiency
	Support basic network administration tasks, including configuring network devices, monitoring connectivity, and troubleshooting network-related issues
	Perform LAN/WAN maintenance, Wi-Fi connectivity support, and internet performance monitoring
	Perform routine system, antivirus, and security updates across workstations and servers
	Support server management tasks, including routine checks, patching, and maintenance activities
	Manage user account setup, password resets, access management, and system permissions administration
	Help maintain IT security standards by supporting firewall, endpoint protection, and access control measures
	Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, and network-related issues for end users
	Support installation, configuration, and maintenance of computer systems, printers, and peripheral devices
	Monitor backup processes and assist in disaster recovery and data restoration activities when required
	Maintain accurate records of IT assets, network configurations, licenses, and access logs
	Maintain documentation for IT processes, policies, procedures, and troubleshooting guides
	Provide first-level technical support.


Software Application Support &amp; Testing:


	Assist in testing software applications before deployment to ensure functionality, usability, and performance
	Document test results, report bugs, and follow up with relevant teams for resolution
	Assist in conducting basic IT orientation and training sessions for staff on company systems, applications, and IT best practices
	Conduct Training on assigned Tech courses during coding bootcamp.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent practical exposure)
	Completion of NYSC or up to 1 year of relevant experience (internship, IT support, network support, training support, or administrative role)
	Good understanding of computer systems, networking concepts, and IT support operations
	Familiarity with TCP/IP, routers, switches, Wi-Fi, and basic network troubleshooting is an added advantage
	Demonstrate considerable competence in network/server administration, cybersecurity practices, hardware support, software testing processes, and IT training delivery
	Good organizational, analytical, and documentation skills
	Strong communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.


Salary
N70,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@sophiaerp.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

        Sophia ERP Limited (SEL) is an Enterprise Solutions Development Organization, on a mission to power Governments and Businesses with cost effective and suitable Digital Transformation solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Network Admin Assistant

Job Location: Ojodu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary

	We are looking to hire a proactive and detail-oriented Network Admin Assistant to support the management, maintenance, and security of our technology infrastructure and daily operations.
	The ideal candidate will assist in ensuring systems and network services run efficiently, support software testing activities, provide timely technical and administrative support to internal teams, and assist with basic IT training initiatives for staff.


Responsibilities
IT & Systems Support:


	Monitor network, server, and system performance to ensure optimal availability and efficiency
	Support basic network administration tasks, including configuring network devices, monitoring connectivity, and troubleshooting network-related issues
	Perform LAN/WAN maintenance, Wi-Fi connectivity support, and internet performance monitoring
	Perform routine system, antivirus, and security updates across workstations and servers
	Support server management tasks, including routine checks, patching, and maintenance activities
	Manage user account setup, password resets, access management, and system permissions administration
	Help maintain IT security standards by supporting firewall, endpoint protection, and access control measures
	Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, and network-related issues for end users
	Support installation, configuration, and maintenance of computer systems, printers, and peripheral devices
	Monitor backup processes and assist in disaster recovery and data restoration activities when required
	Maintain accurate records of IT assets, network configurations, licenses, and access logs
	Maintain documentation for IT processes, policies, procedures, and troubleshooting guides
	Provide first-level technical support.


Software Application Support & Testing:


	Assist in testing software applications before deployment to ensure functionality, usability, and performance
	Document test results, report bugs, and follow up with relevant teams for resolution
	Assist in conducting basic IT orientation and training sessions for staff on company systems, applications, and IT best practices
	Conduct Training on assigned Tech courses during coding bootcamp.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent practical exposure)
	Completion of NYSC or up to 1 year of relevant experience (internship, IT support, network support, training support, or administrative role)
	Good understanding of computer systems, networking concepts, and IT support operations
	Familiarity with TCP/IP, routers, switches, Wi-Fi, and basic network troubleshooting is an added advantage
	Demonstrate considerable competence in network/server administration, cybersecurity practices, hardware support, software testing processes, and IT training delivery
	Good organizational, analytical, and documentation skills
	Strong communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.


Salary
N70,000 - N150,000 per month.

Inventory Officer Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Okpella Plant, Edo

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a dedicated Inventory Officer at Okpella Cement Limited who will be responsible for managing and monitoring store inventory levels, ensuring accurate record-keeping, and supporting smooth supply chain operations.


Key Duties And Responsibilities


	Receive, verify, and record incoming goods and materials.
	Monitor inventory levels and initiate replenishment orders as needed.
	Conduct periodic physical inventory counts and reconcile discrepancies.
	Utilize SAP inventory management modules to track stock movements, process goods receipts, and manage material master data.
	Generate SAP-based inventory reports and ensure all transactions are accurately recorded in the system.
	Update and maintain inventory management systems accurately.
	Prepare regular inventory reports for management review.
	Ensure proper storage, labeling, and handling of all inventory items.
	Coordinate with procurement, logistics, and production teams to support business needs.
	Implement and enforce inventory control policies and procedures.
	Perform any other duties as may be assigned from time to time by Sectional Heads.


Requirements
Education and Work Experience:


	First Degree in Business Administration, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or a related field 
	Minimum of Five (5) years of experience in inventory management or store operations within industrial or cement-related field.


Skills And Competencies:


	Proven experience in inventory management or storekeeping, preferably in a manufacturing or cement industry.
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Proficiency in inventory management software and Microsoft Excel.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.


Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Paid Time Off
	Training & Development.

  "description": "Dangote Group is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard &ndash; earned reputation for excellent business practices and products&rsquo; quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: WordPress IT Administrator

Job Location: Lagos
Department: IT&nbsp;

Job Summary


	We are seeking a skilled and experienced WordPress IT Administrator to join our team.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for performing administrative activities and redesigning multiple WordPress sites to enhance functionality, improve user experience, and ensure optimal performance.


Key Responsibilities
Admin Activities:


	Manage and maintain multiple WordPress sites, ensuring they are up-to-date and secure.
	Perform regular backups, updates, and troubleshooting to ensure site stability and security.
	Monitor site performance and implement optimizations to improve loading times and overall user experience.
	Manage user accounts, permissions, and roles within the WordPress sites.
	Ensure compliance with web standards and best practices.


Redesign Activities:


	Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements and understand the goals for site redesigns.
	Develop and implement new design layouts, themes, and templates to enhance the visual appeal and functionality of the sites.
	Customize and configure WordPress themes and plugins to meet specific project requirements.
	Ensure responsive design and cross-browser compatibility for all redesigned sites.
	Conduct usability testing and gather feedback to make continuous improvements.


&nbsp;Qualifications


	Proven experience as a WordPress IT Administrator or similar role.
	Strong knowledge of WordPress CMS, including themes, plugins, and customizations.
	Proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PHP.
	Experience with website performance optimization and security best practices.
	Familiarity with SEO principles and tools.
	Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
	Strong communication and collaboration skills.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience with graphic design tools such as Adobe Photoshop or Illustrator.
	Knowledge of web analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics).

WordPress IT Administrator Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: WordPress IT Administrator

Job Location: Lagos
Department: IT 

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a skilled and experienced WordPress IT Administrator to join our team.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for performing administrative activities and redesigning multiple WordPress sites to enhance functionality, improve user experience, and ensure optimal performance.


Key Responsibilities
Admin Activities:


	Manage and maintain multiple WordPress sites, ensuring they are up-to-date and secure.
	Perform regular backups, updates, and troubleshooting to ensure site stability and security.
	Monitor site performance and implement optimizations to improve loading times and overall user experience.
	Manage user accounts, permissions, and roles within the WordPress sites.
	Ensure compliance with web standards and best practices.


Redesign Activities:


	Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements and understand the goals for site redesigns.
	Develop and implement new design layouts, themes, and templates to enhance the visual appeal and functionality of the sites.
	Customize and configure WordPress themes and plugins to meet specific project requirements.
	Ensure responsive design and cross-browser compatibility for all redesigned sites.
	Conduct usability testing and gather feedback to make continuous improvements.


 Qualifications


	Proven experience as a WordPress IT Administrator or similar role.
	Strong knowledge of WordPress CMS, including themes, plugins, and customizations.
	Proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PHP.
	Experience with website performance optimization and security best practices.
	Familiarity with SEO principles and tools.
	Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
	Strong communication and collaboration skills.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience with graphic design tools such as Adobe Photoshop or Illustrator.
	Knowledge of web analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics).

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Business Operations (Oil & Gas, EPIC Projects)",
  "description": "Elevare Human Solutions Limited is a forward-thinking HR consulting and workforce solutions company committed to helping organizations build high-performing, people-driven businesses. We partner with companies across industries to deliver strategic and operational HR support that drives productivity, compliance, and sustainable growth. Our core focus areas include talent acquisition, workforce planning, HR advisory, employer branding, and end-to-end people management solutions. We also provide tailored staffing support and operational workforce solutions through our cleaning and facility support services subsidiary, enabling businesses to maintain efficient and well-managed environments.

At Elevare, we understand that people are the most critical asset in any organization. Our approach is rooted in aligning business objectives with the right talent, systems, and culture to create measurable impact. We combine deep HR expertise with practical execution to support both startups and established organizations in building scalable and resilient teams. We are driven by excellence, professionalism, and a strong commitment to delivering value. Our goal is to elevate businesses through strategic human capital solutions that empower growth and long-term success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Business Operations (Oil &amp; Gas, EPIC Projects)

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
